title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface
summary: Teams copy release YAML across repositories; a shared workflow changes an input silently, accepts all secrets, escalates permissions, and hides which workflow revision produced an artifact. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci workflow call typed interface increment in versioned reusable automation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface · Theory Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface · Theory Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface, keep Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface. A defensible solution applies Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned reusable automation contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-workflow-call-typed-interface — Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-4
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: worked gha reuse composite workflow contracts 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reusable workflow inputs accept arbitrary nested objects with runtime type safety. This fails the Ci Workflow Call Typed Interface contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
